stories.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants in retaliation for strait of Hormuz attacks – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Threat from US president comes after US military completes 11th night of strikes on Iran The Jordanian army said it intercepted four Iranian missiles on Wednesday with two additional missile falling in an open area, AFP reports. The two missiles that the army did not intercept fell in “two remote, uninhabited areas, without causing any human casualties or material damage” the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan

stories.title: Video shows death of man in Italy while under police restraint - video
stories.summary: Warning: some viewers may find the following footage distressing. Italian police have clashed with demonstrators in Bologna, northern Italy, during a protest over a man's death. The demonstrations on Monday night followed Italian media reports that Abderrahim Fakir, a Moroccan-born businessman, had died on Sunday when police tried to arrest him. A video filmed by a resident and shared on social media shows Fakir repeatedly calling for help as two officers sit on him and ambulance workers appear

stories.title: Poll: Trump’s MAGA base is souring on his Iran war
stories.summary: Half of President Donald Trump’s MAGA base thought Iran’s war was worth its economic costs in May. Now, just a little more than a third do. That precipitous, 13-point drop in just two months, according to  a new POLITICO Poll , reveals how the president’s most die-hard supporters are starting to agree with the majority of Americans who have long opposed the U.S.’ involvement in the war. Additionally, 37 percent of self-identified MAGA Trump voters say the U.S. should continue its invol

stories.title: Britain’s new defense chief Streeting says Israel has ‘fallen short’ in Gaza
stories.summary: FARNBOROUGH, ENGLAND — Israel has “fallen short” of Britain’s standards of conduct in Gaza and the West Bank, the U.K.’s new Defence Secretary Wes Streeting said Wednesday. Speaking to journalists at the Farnborough International Airshow in one of his first engagements in the job, Streeting said that as a “friend and ally,” Israel should be held to the same standard Britain holds itself to. “That is the standard that I think, I fear, they’ve fallen short of,” he added. stories.title: Russia reports fresh drone strikes on Wildberries logistics hubs
stories.summary: Russia says Ukrainian drone strikes hit Wildberries warehouses for the second time in a week, with drone strikes reportedly triggering major fires and disrupting one of the country's largest e-commerce logistics networks.

stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue re

stories.title: Trump administration says 15 agencies will get $5bn in ‘AI for science’ effort
stories.summary: Administration will also overhaul how US government funds federal research by supporting individual scientists and AI over universities The US will spend $5bn to tackle longstanding scientific problems across multiple fields using AI, ⁠the Trump administration said in a statement on Wednesday. The agencies will use the funding to identify the root causes ⁠of chronic diseases, accelerate drug ⁠discovery and ​develop longer-lasting building materials, among other tasks, according to the statement.

stories.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

stories.title: NHS trust investigated for corporate manslaughter after Nottingham attacks
stories.summary: Trust that treated Valdo Calocane, who killed three people in 2023, sectioned him four times between 2020 and 2022 The NHS trust that treated the triple killer Valdo Calocane in the years before the Nottingham attacks is being investigated by police for corporate manslaughter. stories.title: EU says Trump’s new generic drug tariffs won’t hit its exports
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission said Wednesday that it expects European generic medicines to be exempt from U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest tariffs, arguing that Washington committed to a zero tariff rate with last year’s transatlantic trade deal. Trump on Tuesday announced a plan to impose a 100 percent tariff on generic drugs starting in August 2028 as part of a bid to bring pharmaceutical production back to the United States. stories.title: Iran’s foreign minister describes moment of attack that killed Khamenei
stories.summary: Abbas Araghchi says he has kept on the move since being pulled from rubble after strike on first day of war Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has described how he was pulled from the rubble of a leadership compound after the US-Israeli airstrike that killed the country’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war, and has survived since by constantly moving around the capital, Tehran, by car. stories.title: Norway’s national oil company’s profits double to $11.5bn amid war on Iran
stories.summary: Equinor benefits from decision to increase oil and gas production during strait of Hormuz blockades Business live – latest updates Profits at Norway’s state oil company nearly doubled to $11.5bn (£8.6bn) in the three months to the end of June as the jump in oil and gas prices caused by the war against Iran boosted earnings. stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. stories.summary: As measles, hantavirus and cyclospora spread, experts say lost staff and expertise are slowing the response A series of health challenges – hantavirus , screwworm, Ebola , cyclospora, measles, whooping cough, bird flu – are exposing the widening gaps in America’s ability to prevent, identify and halt infectious disease outbreaks. The rapid dismantling of the US public health system at all levels means the US is in worse shape than it was before the Covid pandemic, former top CDC officials say. C

stories.title: Europe’s heatwave in June cost grain farmers €2bn in lost revenue and crop damage
stories.summary: EU set for for worst crop harvest in decade after total grain yield estimated to be 9m tonnes smaller than expected, analysis finds The heatwave that swept across Europe in June is estimated to have cost the continent’s grain farmers €2bn (£1.7bn) in lost revenue and destroyed 10m tonnes of crops, according to a new analysis, raising concern about the impact on food prices. The European harvest of crops including wheat, barley, maize and oats is now predicted to be 9m tonnes smaller than forecas

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: Outbreak has been traced to Upper East Side cooling towers, but Mamdani says source of exposure has been eliminated A fifth person has died in New York City in connection with the legionnaires’ disease outbreak on Manhattan’s Upper East Side neighborhood, according to the city’s health department. In an update issued Tuesday, the department said that 82 cases of legionnaires’ disease have now been identified and five people have died. stories.title: Zendaya's ancient Iranian earrings stir debate over looted artefacts
stories.summary: Experts said the Iranian 3,000-year-old gold disc medallions Zendaya wore at a promo event for Nolan's Odyssey were "likely looted" and constituted "neocolonialism". Separately, a Zoroastrian symbol worn by IShowSpeed at the World Cup final drew its own debate about Persian cultural identity.

The meeting coincides with ongoing US-mediated talks between Lebanon and Israel. stories.title: Mamdani urges US government to arrest Netanyahu if Israeli PM visits US

stories.title: Ukraine’s drone strikes pile pressure on Russian economy
stories.summary: Russia’s economy is creaking ever more loudly as the cost of financing a fifth year of war continues to mount. The latest sign of strain is a standoff between the Ministry of Finance, which has to raise the money to pay for the war, and the country’s largest banks, which are demanding ever-higher rates to finance it.  The Ministry of Finance said on Monday it would suspend bond sales after a series of auctions failed to attract buyers at the yields local banks were willing to offer. stories.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
stories.summary: Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Macron convinces environment minister to stay on
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron asked his Minister for Ecological Transition Monique Barbut to stay in her role after she resigned on Wednesday morning. “The French president refused her resignation,” government spokesperson Maud Bregeon announced at a press conference. “They agreed on three fundamental pillars that will guide her action: forests, water and environmental health … on which she agreed to remain fully invested.” Barbut’s office told French new

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s plans could bring the UK closer to Europe than he realises
stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
stories.summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
stories.summary: Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open A Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington. The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator. stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

stories.title: Victorious Spanish team parade through Madrid after World Cup triumph
stories.summary: Spain's World Cup-winning team toured Madrid on an open-top bus as huge crowds lined the streets to celebrate their victory over Argentina

stories.title: Iran vows to bomb own land as fears grow over Kuwait incursion
stories.summary: Iranian MPs called for capturing US soldiers and launching ground attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, as discussions grew over a possible ground operation by either side and US strikes entered their 10th consecutive day.

stories.title: Wildfire spreads in southeastern France as firefighters battle blaze
stories.summary: Firefighting aircrafts battled a fast-moving wildfire in southeastern France as the blaze spread across hundreds of hectares, prompting evacuations and threatening nearby communities.

stories.title: Chaos erupts in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat
stories.summary: Riot police use water cannon in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain, as protests break out in the capital.

stories.title: ‘When I get home, my whole body aches’: extreme heat saps health and wages of India’s poorest workers
stories.summary: Exclusive: Outdoor workers losing up to 9% of income as weather becomes ‘poverty multiplier’, research says Extreme heat is pushing some of India’s poorest workers further into poverty, with income losses of up to 9% for outdoor workers, new research has found. Analysis done by the International Institute for Environment and Development (IIED), and shared exclusively with the Guardian, found that extreme heat is acting as a “poverty multiplier” for hundreds of millions of workers in India’s info

stories.title: Trump and Sánchez meet at World Cup final after nearly two years of diplomatic tensions
stories.summary: For nearly two years, the leaders of Spain and the United States have clashed over issues ranging from defence spending to US policy in the Middle East.

stories.title: Watch: Spain fans erupt in celebration after World Cup final victory
stories.summary: Fans in Madrid and Barcelona celebrated long into the night after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time to win their second FIFA World Cup title.

stories.title: US hits Iran for ninth night as Khamenei calls ceasefire 'worthless'
stories.summary: US Central Command said it targeted military command centres, air defence systems and missile sites overnight, as Iran's ayatollah called Trump's signature on the interim deal "worthless and invalid" and a negotiator said Tehran was suspending its commitments.

stories.title: Trump and Infantino hand World Cup trophy to Spain
stories.summary: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino hand the World Cup trophy to Spain captain Rodri after Spain beat Argentina in the final near New York.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup victory
stories.summary: Spain fans celebrate their World Cup final victory over Argentina in Times Square, New York, waving flags, chanting and lifting a replica trophy.

stories.title: Ukraine fires 400 drones at Moscow as Russia steps up ballistic missile attacks
stories.summary: Ukraine's latest attack follows a weekend of escalating exchanges between the two sides, with Russia targeting Kyiv with ballistic missiles on Sunday and Ukraine hitting warehouses outside of Moscow on Saturday.

stories.title: High divers take the plunge at Moscow's Two Rivers festival
stories.summary: High divers from 11 countries compete at Moscow's Two Rivers festival, featuring river jumps, rowing races and hydroflight performances.

stories.title: IRGC pledges 'unforgettable lesson' for US as Iran signals return to talks
stories.summary: The Revolutionary Guard told Ayatollah Khamenei it regarded his message as "a binding command," as Iran's foreign ministry said diplomacy remained open and mediators sought a 10-day ceasefire to revive the framework deal.

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: England beat France 6-4 to claim World Cup third place
stories.summary: Bukayo Saka scored a hat trick as England beat France 6-4 in a record-breaking World Cup third-place playoff. The Three Lions survived a dramatic French comeback to secure their best finish at the tournament since winning the title in 1966.

stories.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition
stories.summary: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami ahead of an extradition request from the United Kingdom. British authorities accuse them of serious offences, including rape, human trafficking and sexual exploitation.

stories.title: Passenger ferry carrying 116 passengers sinks off the coast of Guyana
stories.summary: A distress call by the MV Barima to the coast guard was made just after 11pm local time. The vessel is said to be equipped with 250 life jackets, two rigid life boats and six inflatable life crafts.

stories.title: Magnitude 5.1 earthquake in central Peru leaves at least five dead and ten injured
stories.summary: A 5.1‑magnitude earthquake centred in Chupaca, Junín, left five people dead and around ten injured on Saturday night, and damaged hospitals and religious monuments in the Andean area.

stories.title: 0% intro APR until 2024 is 100% insane

Experts: this is the best cash back card of 2022
Turn Your Rising Home Equity Into Cash You Can Use
Dream Big with a Home Equity Loan

EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
